hero.question: Will Arizona win the 2026 NCAA Tournament?
hero.prob_take: At 21.6%, the market prices Arizona as a tier-2 contender, roughly equivalent to teams that make Elite Eights but rarely cut nets, which feels right for a program that's been to one Final Four in 20 years despite consistent tournament appearances. The +2.2 point bump suggests sharp money is loading up on their 2025-26 roster construction, but without seeing if they keep their current core or suffer portal exodus, you're essentially betting on "Arizona will be good again" at a price that already assumes they will be.

daily_take.headline: Arizona's Tournament Odds Just Spiked. Here's Why Smart Money Should Fade.
daily_take.deck: Arizona jumped 2.2 points overnight to 21.6% as recruiting momentum and transfer portal activity sparked optimism. But the market is confusing "good news now" with "tournament favorite in 18 months." The window to lock in value at 19.4% yesterday is closed. Sharps should wait for the post-season letdown and re-entry at 18-20%.
